description: Curated materials-science case studies for close qualitative inspection. These items exercise fixed score positions and controls, but the set is intentionally too small for paper-scale quantitative claims.

prompts.title: Grain refinement to yield strength
prompts.description: Tests whether the named strengthening law surfaces before a one-word comparative answer.
prompts.text: Two otherwise identical polycrystalline samples have average crystallite diameters of 50 micrometers and 5 micrometers. In the finer sample, interfaces interrupt carrier motion much more frequently. Its yield stress should therefore be
prompts.note: Inspect whether Hall/Petch rises before the answer, whether onset is sustained in the fixed band, and whether its J-lens rank improves materially over the matched logit-lens rank.

prompts.title: Obstacle spacing to bypass stress
prompts.description: A particle-spacing change should evoke the bypass mechanism before the direction of the stress change.
prompts.text: A metal contains a fixed volume fraction of hard, non-shearable nanoscale particles. When their center-to-center spacing is reduced, lattice line defects must bow more sharply between them. The stress required for passage should
prompts.note: Look for Orowan as the specific latent mechanism rather than only generic strengthening. Compare it with the Hall-Petch case to test mechanism specificity.

prompts.title: Rapid quench to martensite
prompts.description: Tests whether transformation-mechanism concepts appear before the product name.
prompts.text: Austenitic steel is cooled so rapidly that long-range atomic redistribution is suppressed. The product is hard and needle-like, and its body-centered unit cell is elongated so that c divided by a is not equal to one. Its crystal symmetry is
prompts.note: The answer is the lattice symmetry; shear and Bain encode the transformation mechanism. Gemma tokenizes martensite into multiple pieces, so the report should explicitly list that exact phase name as dropped rather than silently approximating it.

prompts.title: Resolved stress to slip
prompts.description: An orientation-factor calculation should evoke the named criterion before the deformation event.
prompts.text: A single crystal under uniaxial load has cos(phi) = 0.8 and cos(lambda) = 0.5. Multiplying these orientation factors by the applied stress gives 120 MPa, above the critical resolved value of 80 MPa. The crystal will begin to
prompts.note: Check whether Schmid appears as a law-level concept and whether dislocation appears as the physical carrier before the clean one-token answer.

prompts.title: High-temperature deformation mechanism
prompts.description: The transport path and loading regime imply a named creep mechanism before the transport-process answer.
prompts.text: A fine-grained polycrystal is held near its melting temperature under low stress. Atoms migrate through grain interiors from compressed boundaries toward tensile boundaries, producing slow shape change. The dominant transport process is
prompts.note: Compare generic creep with the more specific Nabarro concept. If only creep surfaces, the representation may be scientifically broad but not mechanism-specific.

prompts.title: Line-defect character
prompts.description: Geometric information should evoke the standard defect vocabulary before the character label.
prompts.text: For a line defect in a crystal, the displacement vector associated with the lattice mismatch is parallel to the defect's line direction. Its character is
prompts.note: This separates recovery of the formal descriptor Burgers from the generic object class dislocation. Inspect their relative onset order rather than only their best ranks.

prompts.title: Diffraction selection-rule recognition
prompts.description: A raw reflection rule must be mapped to a crystal-structure label.
prompts.text: A powder diffraction pattern contains reflections only when h, k, and l are all odd or all even; mixed-parity reflections are systematically absent. The corresponding Bravais lattice is
prompts.note: This is a recognition test rather than a prose association. Check that cubic rises from the extinction rule and is not merely present at the final layer.

prompts.title: Unnamed ductile-fracture vignette
prompts.description: A fractographic description evokes a failure mechanism without naming it.
prompts.text: The tensile specimen formed a pronounced waist before separation. Its failure surface contains rounded microscopic pits, each surrounding a second-phase particle, joined into a cup-and-cone profile.
prompts.note: A strong result distinguishes the specific microvoid/coalescence mechanism from the broader adjective ductile. The generated-output absence control prevents counting a concept the model simply says next.

prompts.title: Unnamed boundary-localized degradation
prompts.description: A processing and microscopy vignette should evoke a specific degradation mode.
prompts.text: An austenitic stainless steel was held in a temperature range where carbides formed at crystallite edges. After exposure to an aggressive solution, attack traces connected paths along those edges while the grain interiors remain largely intact.
prompts.note: Inspect specificity: intergranular plus sensitization is more informative than generic corrosion. Tokenizer-level input checks catch accidental leakage.

prompts.title: Ductile-to-brittle causal counterfactual
prompts.description: Tests whether replacing a latent failure-response coordinate changes the declared downstream answer.
prompts.text: A pure metal has many easy close-packed slip systems and accommodates extensive glide before separation. In a tensile test beyond yield it normally shows substantial
prompts.note: Grade the clean and counterfactual output tokens. A target-rank improvement without the declared answer change is not causal success.

prompts.title: Focus on a dislocation pile-up
prompts.description: Teacher-forced carrier with a materials side concept.
prompts.user: Write "The blue notebook remained beside the furnace." Concentrate on a dislocation pile-up and its stress concentration while you write the sentence. Do not write anything else.
prompts.assistant_prefill: The blue notebook remained beside the furnace.
prompts.note: Compare only against the matched suppress and neutral records; the absolute rank by itself is not the modulation result.

prompts.title: Suppress a dislocation pile-up
prompts.description: Matched suppress condition using the identical carrier and target set.
prompts.user: Write "The blue notebook remained beside the furnace." Try not to think about a dislocation pile-up or its stress concentration while you write the sentence. Do not write anything else.
prompts.assistant_prefill: The blue notebook remained beside the furnace.

prompts.title: Neutral carrier control
prompts.description: Matched carrier with no materials side task.
prompts.user: Write "The blue notebook remained beside the furnace." Do not write anything else.
prompts.assistant_prefill: The blue notebook remained beside the furnace.

prompts.title: Crystal-defect verbal-report swap
prompts.description: Dynamically select the clean one-word report, then try sibling defect coordinates.
prompts.user: Think of a crystalline defect. Answer in one word.
prompts.note: Inspect every recorded candidate trial. The primary diagnostic is whether the one-word report changes to the installed sibling, not whether its internal rank merely improves.
